topic: Sui Move contract authoring (Move 2024 edition, mainnet toolchain)

coverage.note: not recorded — coverage completeness is unverified

falsification.question: Held-out Sui Move contract tasks - does the pack improve output quality on the pinned toolchain (sui 1.74.1-8fc60f1fa966)?

experiment_1.verdict: ceiling - metric could not discriminate; did not clear the ship rule

experiment_2.metrics: compile-pass (primary) + zero-warning clean-compile (secondary), pre-registered before answers at commit b9653b6
experiment_2.verdict: primary tie, secondary kp wins -> ships under pre-registered rule branch 2

experiment_3.metrics: compile-pass (primary) + zero-warning clean-compile (secondary), 6 held-out tasks, claude-haiku-4-5 all arms, pre-registered before answers at commit b5c9fe9; headline comparison = deepened pack (kp61, 8c1a2e5) vs pre-deepening pack (kp47, 82d55df)
experiment_3.verdict: kp61 > kp47 on the primary -> deepening improves the pack, ships under pre-registered rule branch 1; secondary tied 1/6 all arms (no cleanliness claim). The taught use-self corner (E03003) recurred twice in the kp47 arm and zero taught-class failures appeared in kp61. Disclosed correction: one task (escrow-swap) overlaps experiment 1's task 4, contrary to the pre-registered held-out assertion; excluding it the primary is kp61 4/5 vs kp47 2/5 - verdict unchanged.

experiment_4.metrics: compile-pass (primary) + zero-warning clean-compile (secondary), 6 held-out tasks, claude-haiku-4-5 all arms, pre-registered before answers at commit d5947f6; headline comparison = round-2 pack (kp86) vs pre-round-2 pack (kp61) vs base
experiment_4.verdict: NO HEADLINE - kp86 tied kp61 on both metrics, ship rule lands on branch 3; the base arm beat both pack arms on the primary (5/6 vs 3/6). The taught option-field-fill class fired only in the kp61 arm (both affected tasks passed under kp86) and no taught error class from any round recurred in kp86; the pack arms lost their gains to new import-path and API-existence classes, several plausibly induced by the pack's own idiom vocabulary (recorded as an observation, not a measured mechanism).

experiment_5.metrics: compile-pass (primary) + zero-warning clean-compile (secondary), 6 held-out tasks, claude-haiku-4-5 all arms, pre-registered before answers at commit 137b267; headline comparison = round-3 pack (kp98) vs pre-round-3 pack (kp86) vs base; also pre-registered: the vocabulary-induction observational analysis
experiment_5.verdict: NO HEADLINE - kp98 tied kp86 on the primary (3/6 = 3/6), ship rule lands on branch 3, and kp98 REGRESSED on the secondary (clean-compile 1/6 vs 3/6): all three lost-cleanliness warnings are Lint W99001, the class the loaded round-2 transfer-composability beat teaches. Both pack arms beat base on the primary (3/6 vs 2/6; the flipped task failed in base on the original taught struct-visibility class). Zero error-tier taught classes recurred in kp98 at the fixture-rule scope (two of its failures were loaded-rule-adjacent - same message template, different pinned rule and fix; recurrence standard stated in the experiment README), but kp86 recorded three loaded-rule-ignored classes (use-self, option-field-fill, block-statement-semicolon); the pre-registered vocabulary-induction analysis fit 0 of 6 failing pack-arm rows - experiment 4's post-hoc 3-of-6 fit did not replicate. Recorded forks for the next unit of work: a small round 4 (SUI-import + Rust-bleed + hash corner) or a payload-salience/slimming experiment.

payload_salience.metrics: compile-pass (primary) + zero-warning clean-compile (secondary), 6 fresh held-out tasks, claude-haiku-4-5 all arms, pre-registered before answers at commit c170058; same 98 claims (master 04d7cba) in every pack arm - only the payload FORM varies: full (standard CONTEXT.md + sorted claim statements, 49656 B, byte-identical to experiment 5's kp98 payload), slim (same sorted statements once, briefing stripped, 24639 B), sheet (35 imperative rule lines derived 1:1 from the claims, 5887 B); headline comparisons = slim vs full and sheet vs full (disclosed multiplicity, >= +2 bar)
payload_salience.verdict: BRANCH 1 - payload form matters: sheet 3/6 >= full 1/6 + 2 (bar met exactly, zero margin, n=6, two disclosed comparisons); the sheet is adopted as the recommended answer-time load format (the full payload remains the archival/verification form). The slim control scored 0/6, below full, so the win is the sheet's imperative form and/or compression (a confound disclosed at pre-registration), NOT redundancy removal - mechanical slimming did not help. Secondary: no treatment cleared anything (sheet's three passes all carry warnings; the sheet buys compile-pass, not cleanliness). Hardest draw of the series (base 0/6; full 1/6 vs its 3/6 history) - absolute numbers are not comparable across experiments. The pre-registered mechanism analysis did NOT cleanly support the salience hypothesis: the sheet's three failing rows carry three loaded-rule-ignored events with exact pinned messages (struct-field-store, implicit-field-copy, the create-currency call shape), so form buys passes without solving rule application. Required before emit-sheet tooling: one confirmation draw, sheet vs full only (one comparison).

falsification.verdict: pack improves cleanliness/modern-idiom compliance (pre-registered clean-compile: kp 3/5 vs base 2/5); it does NOT raise raw compile-pass for claude-sonnet-4-6 (tie both experiments). Known gap at falsification time: transfer::receive/Receiving<T> (both arms failed identically, Sui E02009). Gap closed 2026-07-07 by the fixture-proven receiving beat added AFTER the experiment; the recorded numbers were measured on the 44-claim pack. Deepening round 1 (2026-07-07, docs/deepening/round-1/) added 14 more claims after these experiments; experiment 3 (pre-registered, held-out) then measured the deepening delta for claude-haiku-4-5: deepened pack 4/6 compile-pass vs pre-deepening pack 2/6 -> deepening improves the pack (ship rule branch 1). Each experiment's numbers stay pinned to the pack size and model they measured. Deepening round 2 (2026-07-07, docs/deepening/round-2/) added 25 more claims (61 -> 86) AFTER experiment 3. Experiment 4 (pre-registered, held-out) then measured round 2's delta for claude-haiku-4-5: NO HEADLINE - kp86 tied kp61 on both metrics and the base arm beat both pack arms on the primary (5/6 vs 3/6). Deepening round 3 (2026-07-08, docs/deepening/round-3/) added 12 more claims (86 -> 98) AFTER experiment 4; Experiment 5 (pre-registered, held-out) then measured round 3's delta for claude-haiku-4-5: NO HEADLINE - kp98 tied kp86 on compile-pass (3/6 = 3/6) and regressed on clean-compile (1/6 vs 3/6, all three warnings the loaded transfer-composability class); both pack arms beat base on the primary (3/6 vs 2/6). Two consecutive no-headline experiments: the recorded next forks are a small targeted round 4 (SUI-import + Rust-bleed + hash corner) or a payload-salience/slimming experiment on the existing 98 claims. The payload-salience experiment (2026-07-08, docs/experiments/sui-payload-salience/) then tested form on the frozen 98 claims: sheet form 3/6 compile-pass vs full payload 1/6 (ship rule branch 1, bar met exactly), slim 0/6, base 0/6 - payload form matters; the ~6 KB imperative sheet is the recommended answer-time load format pending a pre-registered confirmation draw (sheet vs full only), and the ignored-while-loaded phenomenon persisted in every payload form tested.
